seoTitle: Port Albert vs Port Fairy — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Port Albert and Port Fairy. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Port Albert vs Port Fairy: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Port Albert and Port Fairy.
heading: Port Albert vs Port Fairy — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Port Albert, Victoria and Port Fairy, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Port Fairy has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Port Albert (38/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Port Albert or Port Fairy safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Port Albert has a flood risk score of 47/100, while Port Fairy scores 48/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Port Albert scores 62/100 for bushfire risk. Port Fairy scores 20/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Port Albert or Port Fairy?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Port Fairy has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.25% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
